cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question. The question is asking what we know about garage sales based on the provided text. The options are (A) It is only held in the spring of the year. (B) The goods on display are totally useless. (C) Garage sales are popular with Americans. (D) All the goods are selling far below their real value. Now, I'll go through the text to find the correct answer. The text says, "Perhaps even more enjoyable than flea markets are the weekend outdoor garage sales which have now become very much a part of American culture." This implies that garage sales are a popular part of American culture, so option (C) seems correct. Option (A) is incorrect because the text states garage sales are held usually in the spring and fall, not only in the spring. Option (B) is incorrect because the text says the goods are in good condition and great buys can be found, implying they are not totally useless.
